prp.objective: Create internal/generate/dedupe.go — the FIRST file in the NEW `package generate` — exporting two PURE, stdlib-only helper functions that implement the OUTER duplicate-rejection seam of the two-nested-loop CommitStaged orchestrator (PRD FR30-FR33; decisions.md §3): `func firstLine(msg string) string` (the generated-message subject = the first '\n'-delimited line of msg, strings.TrimSpace'd — ports the reference `subject = head -1 commit_msg`, reference_impl.md §1/§2; PRD FR30) and `func isDuplicate(subject string, subjects []string) bool` (an EXACT, case-sensitive membership test of subject against the []string returned by git.RecentSubjects(50) — PRD FR31/FR32 "exactly matches one of the 50"; fuzzy/Levenshtein is explicitly v1.1, NOT here). Plus internal/generate/dedupe_test.go — white-box `package generate`, stdlib `testing` (+ `strings`) ONLY, table-driven subtests covering the contract MOCKING scenarios: exact match true; near/paraphrased match false (fuzzy deferred); case-difference false; internal-whitespace-difference false; firstLine of a multi-line message returns only line 1; leading/trailing whitespace ignored via firstLine's trim (proved end-to-end: whitespace-bearing first line -> firstLine trims -> isDuplicate exact-matches); plus CRLF/empty/nil/empty-slice edge cases. This supplies isDuplicate + firstLine for the generate outer loop consumer (P1.M6.T1.S1 generate.go, which depends on this task).
prp.context:
# PRP — P1.M6.T1.S2: internal/generate/dedupe.go — isDuplicate + firstLine

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Add the duplicate-rejection helpers that sit at the bottom of the generate OUTER loop (decisions.md §3): `firstLine` extracts the generated-message subject (first line, trimmed) and `isDuplicate` performs an EXACT, case-sensitive membership test against the recent-subjects set. These are PURE functions over `string` / `[]string` (stdlib `strings` ONLY) — no git binary, no I/O, no testify — faithfully porting the reference `subject = head -1 commit_msg` / `reject if in git log --format=%s -50` mechanics (reference_impl.md §1 step 4 + §2; PRD FR30-FR32). Fuzzy/Levenshtein dedupe is explicitly DEFERRED to v1.1 (decisions.md §3 RESEARCH NOTE; work-item description) — v1 ships byte-exact, case-sensitive matching only.

**Deliverable**: TWO NEW files creating the `internal/generate` package (which does NOT yet exist — this is its first file):
- `internal/generate/dedupe.go` — `func firstLine(msg string) string` and `func isDuplicate(subject string, subjects []string) bool`. Imports ONLY stdlib `strings`. Uses a PLAIN `package generate` line preceded by an adjacent file-level comment that DEFERS the `// Package generate` doc to generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1) — the EXACT precedent of internal/git/log.go deferring to git.go and internal/prompt/system.go/payload.go deferring to examples.go.
- `internal/generate/dedupe_test.go` — white-box `package generate` (NOT `package generate_test`), stdlib `testing` + `strings` ONLY, table-driven subtests (one `Test*` per behavior family using `t.Run(tc.name, ...)` with `tc := tc` capture), asserting exact return values with `t.Errorf("... = %q, want %q", got, want)` / `%v` for bools.

**Success Definition**: `go build ./internal/generate/`, `go vet ./internal/generate/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/generate/` empty; `go test ./internal/generate/` PASS (all firstLine + isDuplicate table cases + the end-to-end trim-then-match proof); `go test ./...` whole-module green (existing config/git/prompt/provider/ui unaffected). stdlib-only imports (`strings` in dedupe.go; `testing`+`strings` in dedupe_test.go); NO go.mod/go.sum change; no `go mod tidy`; no go-git, no testify, no real git binary. Only the two new files created; no other packages touched. Mode A docs satisfied (godoc on both functions cites PRD FR30-FR33, decisions.md §3, reference_impl.md §1/§2). generate.go (S1), rescue.go (S3), and any orchestrator/signal/integration code are NOT created here.

## Why

- **This is the OUTER loop's gate (the product's anti-duplicate guarantee).** The generate orchestrator (S1, decisions.md §3) runs `subject = firstLine(msg); if !isDuplicate(subject, recentSubjects) { goto COMMIT } else { rejected = append(rejected, subject); outer-retry }`. Getting these two primitives byte-faithful to FR30-FR33 is what makes Stagehand NEVER reuse an existing commit subject — the exact defensibility claim in PRD §13. firstLine feeds isDuplicate; isDuplicate decides retry-vs-commit.
- **EXACT match is a deliberate v1 boundary.** FR31/FR32 say "exactly matches". Fuzzy/Levenshtein matching is explicitly punted to v1.1 (work-item RESEARCH NOTE; decisions.md §3). Shipping a pure `==` (byte-equal, case-sensitive) keeps v1 deterministic, trivially testable, and free of any string-distance dependency. The table tests MUST assert that NEAR/paraphrased subjects (e.g. "add parsers" vs "add parser", "refactor the parser" vs "refactor parser") return FALSE — so a future v1.1 fuzzy pass has a regression baseline proving the behavior CHANGED intentionally.
- **Trim responsibility is centralized, not smeared.** The architecture (decisions.md §3 + reference_impl.md §1) trims in TWO well-defined places: `firstLine` trims the generated subject, and `git.RecentSubjects` (the shipped dependency P1.M3.T4.S1) already trims each history subject. So `isDuplicate` performs NO trimming — it is a pure EXACT `==`. This keeps isDuplicate trivially correct and makes the "leading/trailing whitespace ignored via trim" MOCKING scenario a property of firstLine (proved end-to-end: a whitespace-bearing first line passes through firstLine's trim and THEN exact-matches a history subject).
- **Pure functions = the easiest possible unit under test.** No git binary, no exec, no filesystem, no time — just `string` in, `string`/`bool` out. This mirrors the codebase's pure-function table-test precedents (internal/provider/parse.go + parse_test.go for parseOutput; internal/prompt/payload.go + payload_test.go for AssemblePayload): white-box same-package, stdlib testing only, `t.Run` subtests.

## What (technical requirements)

1. NEW file `internal/generate/dedupe.go`. FIRST line a PLAIN `package generate` (because this is the package's first file but generate.go P1.M6.T1.S1 will OWN the `// Package generate` doc — mirror internal/git/log.go's deferral-to-git.go posture EXACTLY: a file-level comment immediately above `package generate` explaining THIS file adds firstLine + isDuplicate for the generate outer loop and noting it defers the package doc to generate.go). Imports: stdlib ONLY — `strings`. NO go-git, NO testify, NO new dep (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum).
2. **`firstLine`** — `func firstLine(msg string) string`:
   - `if i := strings.IndexByte(msg, '\n'); i != -1 { msg = msg[:i] }` (take the substring up to the FIRST newline; if there is no newline, keep the whole string — this ports `head -1`).
   - `return strings.TrimSpace(msg)` (trim leading/trailing whitespace from that first line).
   - CRLF is handled for free: for "x\r\nbody", IndexByte('\n') yields the line "x\r" and TrimSpace strips the trailing '\r' (it is whitespace) -> "x". Empty input -> ""; a whitespace-only first line ("   \nbody") -> ""; "trailing\n" -> "trailing".
   - godoc cites PRD FR30 ("Extract the generated subject (first line of the message)"), decisions.md §3 (`subject = firstLine(msg)`), reference_impl.md §1 step 4 + §2 (`subject = head -1 commit_msg`), and states the trim responsibility + that it feeds isDuplicate.
3. **`isDuplicate`** — `func isDuplicate(subject string, subjects []string) bool`:
   - A plain linear scan: `for _, s := range subjects { if s == subject { return true } }; return false`.
   - EXACT, case-sensitive `==` (byte-equal). NO trimming, NO lowercasing, NO normalization, NO Levenshtein (FR31/FR32 "exactly matches"; fuzzy is v1.1). The 50-element max input makes O(n) trivially fine.
   - `nil` slice and empty slice both yield false (the range is empty). `subject == ""` yields false unless "" is literally an element (RecentSubjects drops empties, so in practice never).
   - godoc cites PRD FR31 ("Fetch the last 50 commit subjects"), FR32 ("If the subject exactly matches one of the 50"), decisions.md §3 (`if !isDuplicate(subject, recentSubjects) { goto COMMIT }`), reference_impl.md §1/§2 (the dedupe reject-in-`git log --format=%s -50`), the dependency git.RecentSubjects(50) (P1.M3.T4.S1), the EXACT case-sensitive contract, and the explicit fuzzy-deferred-to-v1.1 boundary. State the trim-responsibility boundary: callers MUST pass already-trimmed strings (firstLine trims the subject; git.RecentSubjects trims its elements) — isDuplicate trims NOTHING.
4. NEW file `internal/generate/dedupe_test.go`, `package generate` (WHITE-BOX — it references the unexported... NOTE firstLine/isDuplicate ARE exported, but the house convention for pure-function unit tests in this codebase is same-package white-box, matching internal/provider/parse_test.go and internal/prompt/payload_test.go; follow it). stdlib `testing` + `strings` ONLY (NO testify, NO real git, NO internal/git import — these are pure string functions). Do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum. Use table-driven subtests: `tests := []struct{...}`; `for _, tc := range tests { tc := tc; t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T){...}) }`; assert with `t.Errorf("firstLine(%q) = %q, want %q", tc.msg, got, tc.want)` / `t.Errorf("isDuplicate(%q, %v) = %v, want %v", tc.subject, tc.subjects, got, tc.want)`.
5. Test cases (cover EVERY MOCKING bullet from the contract):
   - `TestFirstLine` table: (a) single line no newline -> whole string trimmed; (b) ★ multi-line -> ONLY line 1 trimmed (e.g. "feat: multi\n\nFirst body.\nSecond body." -> "feat: multi"); (c) ★ leading/trailing whitespace on line 1 -> trimmed ("  feat: trimmed  " -> "feat: trimmed"); (d) CRLF ("feat: crlf\r\nbody" -> "feat: crlf"); (e) empty "" -> ""; (f) whitespace-only first line ("   \nbody") -> ""; (g) trailing-newline-only ("feat: x\n") -> "feat: x".
   - `TestIsDuplicate` table: (a) ★ exact match -> true; (b) no match -> false; (c) ★ case differs ("Feat: Add Parser" vs "feat: add parser") -> false (case-sensitive); (d) ★ near/paraphrase -> false (TWO sub-cases: "feat: add parsers" vs "feat: add parser"; and "refactor the parser module" vs "refactor parser module" — proves fuzzy is NOT done); (e) internal whitespace differs ("feat: add  parser" [two spaces] vs "feat: add parser" [one]) -> false (exact = byte-equal); (f) empty subjects slice -> false; (g) nil subjects slice -> false; (h) empty subject "" vs non-empty list -> false.
   - `TestDedup_FirstLineThenIsDuplicate_WhitespaceTrimmed` (the END-TO-END MOCKING proof): take a multi-line message whose first line has leading/trailing whitespace ("  feat: add parser  \n\nBody line."), compute `subj := firstLine(msg)`, assert `subj == "feat: add parser"`, then assert `isDuplicate(subj, []string{"feat: add parser", "fix: bug"}) == true` — proving the "leading/trailing whitespace ignored via trim" + "firstLine of a multi-line message returns only line 1" + "exact match true" pipeline the orchestrator (S1) will rely on. ALSO assert a NON-matching multi-line message ("docs: update readme\nbody") against the SAME recent set returns false.
6. **Scope / boundary**: ONLY create `internal/generate/dedupe.go` and `internal/generate/dedupe_test.go` (creating the new `internal/generate` directory). Do NOT create generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1 — the CommitStaged orchestrator), rescue.go (P1.M6.T1.S3), or any signal/timeout/integration-harness code (M6.T2/T3). Do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum, Makefile, main.go, or any existing package (config/git/prompt/provider/ui). Do NOT run `go mod tidy`. Do NOT import go-git/testify/any dep — stdlib only. The `// Package generate` doc is deferred to generate.go (S1); dedupe.go uses a plain `package generate` line (the deferral precedent is internal/git/log.go).

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models / structure
```go
package generate
import "strings"
func firstLine(msg string) string {
	if i := strings.IndexByte(msg, '\n'); i != -1 {
		msg = msg[:i]
	}
	return strings.TrimSpace(msg)
}
func isDuplicate(subject string, subjects []string) bool {
	for _, s := range subjects {
		if s == subject {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

```

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** Per the work item: godoc on `firstLine` cites PRD FR30 ("Extract the generated subject (first line of the message)") + decisions.md §3 (`subject = firstLine(msg)`) + reference_impl.md §1 step 4 / §2 (`subject = head -1 commit_msg`); godoc on `isDuplicate` cites PRD FR31 ("last 50 commit subjects") + FR32 ("exactly matches one of the 50") + decisions.md §3 (the `if !isDuplicate(subject, recentSubjects) { goto COMMIT }` call site) + reference_impl.md §1/§2 (reject if in `git log --format=%s -50`), names the dependency git.RecentSubjects(50) (P1.M3.T4.S1) as the `subjects` source, states the EXACT case-sensitive contract, and explicitly flags fuzzy/Levenshtein as deferred to v1.1. This is the ONLY documentation this task produces (inline godoc). Do NOT create README.md, docs/, or providers/*.toml here. This PRP surfaces the item's DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT add trimming (TrimSpace), lowercasing, or Levenshtein inside isDuplicate — it is a pure EXACT, case-sensitive `==` (FR31/FR32 "exactly matches"). Trimming is firstLine's + git.RecentSubjects's job; isDuplicate trims NOTHING.
- ❌ Do NOT implement fuzzy/substring/Levenshtein matching here — it is explicitly v1.1 (decisions.md §3 RESEARCH NOTE). Near/paraphrased subjects MUST return false (the table tests assert this so a v1.1 fuzzy pass has a regression baseline).
- ❌ Do NOT use strings.Split for firstLine — it allocates and mishandles trailing-newline/empty. Use strings.IndexByte(msg, '\n') (allocation-free head-1), then TrimSpace.
- ❌ Do NOT make firstLine return the whole multi-line message — it returns ONLY the first line trimmed (PRD FR30 / head -1).
- ❌ Do NOT make dedupe_test.go `package generate_test` or import testify/internal/git — match the house white-box pure-function convention (`package generate`, stdlib testing+strings only; precedents: parse_test.go, payload_test.go). These are pure string functions; no real git binary is needed.
- ❌ Do NOT own the `// Package generate` doc in dedupe.go — defer it to generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1) via a plain `package generate` line + adjacent file-level comment (the EXACT internal/git/log.go-defers-to-git.go precedent; verified gofmt+vet clean).
- ❌ Do NOT create generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1), rescue.go (P1.M6.T1.S3), or any orchestrator/signal/timeout/integration-harness code — those are SEPARATE work items.
- ❌ Do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum, Makefile, main.go, or any existing package (config/git/prompt/provider/ui). Do NOT run `go mod tidy`. Stdlib only.
- ❌ Do NOT forget the `tc := tc` capture in the table-test range loop (the codehouse convention).
implementationSteps: Create the NEW directory `internal/generate/` and the NEW file `internal/generate/dedupe.go`. Put a leading file-level comment IMMEDIATELY above (NO blank line) the PLAIN `package generate` line, explaining THIS file adds firstLine + isDuplicate — the duplicate-rejection primitives for the generate OUTER loop (PRD FR30-FR33; decisions.md §3) — and that it uses a plain `package generate` line because generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1) OWNS the `// Package generate` doc, mirroring how internal/git/log.go defers to git.go (verified gofmt+vet clean). Import ONLY stdlib `strings` (NO go-git/testify/new dep; do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum).

Implement `func firstLine(msg string) string`: `if i := strings.IndexByte(msg, '\n'); i != -1 { msg = msg[:i] }; return strings.TrimSpace(msg)`. Use IndexByte (allocation-free head-1) — do NOT use strings.Split. CRLF is handled for free (TrimSpace strips a trailing '\r'). Add a godoc citing PRD FR30 ("Extract the generated subject (first line of the message)"), decisions.md §3 (`subject = firstLine(msg)`), reference_impl.md §1 step 4 + §2 (`subject = head -1 commit_msg`), and note it trims the subject that feeds isDuplicate.

Implement `func isDuplicate(subject string, subjects []string) bool`: a linear scan `for _, s := range subjects { if s == subject { return true } }; return false` — an EXACT, case-sensitive, byte-equal `==` (PRD FR31/FR32 "exactly matches"). NO trimming, NO lowercasing, NO Levenshtein (fuzzy is v1.1). Add a godoc citing PRD FR31 ("last 50 commit subjects") + FR32 ("exactly matches one of the 50"), decisions.md §3 (the `if !isDuplicate(subject, recentSubjects) { goto COMMIT }` call site), reference_impl.md §1/§2 (reject if in `git log --format=%s -50`), the dependency git.RecentSubjects(50) (P1.M3.T4.S1) as the subjects source, the EXACT case-sensitive contract, the fuzzy-deferred-to-v1.1 boundary, AND the trim-responsibility statement (isDuplicate trims NOTHING — callers firstLine + git.RecentSubjects pre-trim both sides).

Create `internal/generate/dedupe_test.go` as `package generate` (WHITE-BOX — match internal/provider/parse_test.go + internal/prompt/payload_test.go house convention; do NOT be `package generate_test`). Import ONLY stdlib `testing` + `strings`. NO testify, NO internal/git import, NO real git binary. Use table-driven subtests: `tests := []struct{...}`; `for _, tc := range tests { tc := tc; t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T){ ... }) }`; assert with `t.Errorf("firstLine(%q) = %q, want %q", tc.msg, got, tc.want)` and `t.Errorf("isDuplicate(%q, %v) = %v, want %v", tc.subject, tc.subjects, got, tc.want)`.

Add `TestFirstLine` table covering: single line no newline -> whole string trimmed; ★multi-line -> ONLY line 1 trimmed ("feat: multi

First body.
Second body." -> "feat: multi"); ★leading/trailing whitespace on line 1 -> trimmed ("  feat: trimmed  " -> "feat: trimmed"); CRLF ("feat: crlf
body" -> "feat: crlf"); empty "" -> ""; whitespace-only first line ("   
body") -> ""; trailing-newline-only ("feat: x
") -> "feat: x".

Add `TestIsDuplicate` table covering: ★exact match -> true; no match -> false; ★case differs ("Feat: Add Parser" vs "feat: add parser") -> false (case-sensitive); ★near/paraphrase -> false (TWO sub-cases: "feat: add parsers" vs "feat: add parser"; and "refactor the parser module" vs "refactor parser module" — proves fuzzy is NOT done); internal whitespace differs ("feat: add  parser" [two spaces] vs "feat: add parser" [one]) -> false (exact = byte-equal); empty subjects slice -> false; nil subjects slice -> false; empty subject "" vs non-empty list -> false.

Add `TestDedup_FirstLineThenIsDuplicate_WhitespaceTrimmed` — the END-TO-END MOCKING proof: a multi-line message whose first line has leading/trailing whitespace ("  feat: add parser  

Body line.") -> compute `subj := firstLine(msg)`, assert `subj == "feat: add parser"`, then assert `isDuplicate(subj, []string{"feat: add parser", "fix: bug"}) == true` (proving leading/trailing whitespace ignored via firstLine's trim, multi-line returns line 1, then exact-match true). ALSO assert a NON-matching multi-line message ("docs: update readme
body") against the SAME recent set returns false.

Run the validation gates IN ORDER and fix any failure before declaring done: `go build ./internal/generate/` (compiles); `go vet ./internal/generate/` (clean — this gate also compiles _test.go); `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/generate/)"` (no unformatted files); `go test ./internal/generate/` (PASS — all firstLine + isDuplicate table cases + the end-to-end proof); `go test ./...` (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; config + git + prompt + provider + ui unaffected).

DOCS impact: Mode A — confirm firstLine + isDuplicate each carry a godoc citing PRD FR30-FR33, decisions.md §3, and reference_impl.md §1/§2 (isDuplicate also names git.RecentSubjects(50) and the fuzzy-deferred-to-v1.1 boundary). Create NO README/docs/providers TOML. Confirm scope boundaries: ONLY internal/generate/dedupe.go and internal/generate/dedupe_test.go were created (the new internal/generate directory); go.mod/go.sum/Makefile/main.go + internal/config, internal/git, internal/prompt, internal/provider, internal/ui are unchanged; only stdlib imports were added (strings; testing+strings; no go-git/testify); no `go mod tidy` was run; generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1) and rescue.go (P1.M6.T1.S3) were NOT created; the file uses a plain `package generate` line deferring the `// Package generate` doc to generate.go.

successCriteria.description: internal/generate/dedupe.go exists (creating the NEW internal/generate package), starts with a PLAIN `package generate` line preceded by an adjacent file-level comment DEFERRING the `// Package generate` doc to generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1) — mirroring internal/git/log.go deferring to git.go — and imports ONLY stdlib `strings` (no go-git, no testify, no new dep; go.mod/go.sum unchanged).

firstLine(msg string) string is implemented as `if i := strings.IndexByte(msg, '\n'); i != -1 { msg = msg[:i] }; return strings.TrimSpace(msg)` (head-1 + trim; allocation-free IndexByte, NOT strings.Split) and carries godoc citing PRD FR30, decisions.md §3, reference_impl.md §1/§2.

isDuplicate(subject string, subjects []string) bool is implemented as a pure EXACT, case-sensitive, byte-equal `==` linear scan (NO trimming/lowercasing/Levenshtein; fuzzy explicitly deferred to v1.1) and carries godoc citing PRD FR31/FR32, decisions.md §3, reference_impl.md §1/§2, naming git.RecentSubjects(50) (P1.M3.T4.S1) as the subjects source and stating the trim-responsibility boundary (isDuplicate trims NOTHING; callers pre-trim).

internal/generate/dedupe_test.go is white-box `package generate`, stdlib `testing`+`strings` ONLY (no testify, no internal/git import, no real git binary), uses table-driven `t.Run(tc.name,...)` subtests with `tc := tc` capture, and PASSES the contract MOCKING scenarios: firstLine (single-line, multi-line->line-1, leading/trailing-whitespace trim, CRLF, empty, whitespace-only-first-line, trailing-newline) and isDuplicate (exact-match-true, no-match-false, case-differs-false, near/paraphrase-false [fuzzy NOT done], internal-whitespace-differs-false, empty-slice-false, nil-slice-false, empty-subject-false).

An end-to-end test proves the orchestrator's intended pipeline: a multi-line message with a whitespace-bearing first line passes through firstLine (trimmed) and isDuplicate then EXACT-matches a history subject (true), while a non-matching multi-line message returns false — covering the 'leading/trailing whitespace ignored via trim' + 'firstLine of a multi-line message returns only line 1' + 'exact match true' MOCKING bullets together.

go build ./internal/generate/ and go vet ./internal/generate/ are clean; gofmt -l internal/generate/ produces no output; go test ./internal/generate/ exits 0 (PASS); go test ./... exits 0 (whole-module integrity; Makefile `test` target green; internal/config, internal/git, internal/prompt, internal/provider, internal/ui tests unaffected).

Mode A docs satisfied: godoc on firstLine + isDuplicate cites PRD FR30-FR33, decisions.md §3, reference_impl.md §1/§2 (isDuplicate also names git.RecentSubjects(50) and the fuzzy-deferred-to-v1.1 boundary). Scope boundaries respected: only internal/generate/dedupe.go and internal/generate/dedupe_test.go created; go.mod/go.sum/Makefile/main.go + internal/config, internal/git, internal/prompt, internal/provider, internal/ui unchanged; no README/docs/providers TOML created; no `go mod tidy`; generate.go (P1.M6.T1.S1) and rescue.go (P1.M6.T1.S3) NOT created; no go-git/testify import.

references: PRD.md §9.7 FR30 — "Extract the generated subject (first line of the message)." Pins firstLine's contract (first line, trimmed). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §9.7 FR31 — "Fetch the last 50 commit subjects (git log --format=%s -50)." This []string is the isDuplicate haystack, supplied by git.RecentSubjects(50) (P1.M3.T4.S1). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §9.7 FR32 — "If the subject EXACTLY matches one of the 50, retry..." Pins isDuplicate's EXACT, case-sensitive contract; the word 'exactly' rules out fuzzy/substring (deferred to v1.1). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §9.7 FR33 — "On exhausting duplicate retries, enter the rescue path." Context for WHY isDuplicate exists (the orchestrator S1 wires retry/rescue; isDuplicate is just the boolean gate; rescue is S3, NOT here). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §3 — THE two-nested-loop orchestrator pseudocode showing the exact call sites: `subject = firstLine(msg)` then `if !isDuplicate(subject, recentSubjects) { goto COMMIT } else { rejected = append(rejected, subject) }`. Pins the signatures firstLine(msg string) string + isDuplicate(subject string, subjects []string) bool and the trim-responsibility boundary (firstLine trims; RecentSubjects trims; isDuplicate trims nothing). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §9 porting map — maps the duplicate-retry loop to internal/generate/dedupe.go + generate.go (OUTER loop wrapping INNER parse loop); confirms dedupe.go is the home for firstLine + isDuplicate and the loop control lives in generate.go (S1). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/reference_impl.md §1 + §2 — the proven mechanics to port: subject = head -1 commit_msg (firstLine); reject if subject in `git log --format=%s -50` (isDuplicate). §2 is the two-nested-loop behavioral baseline (outer dup loop wrapping inner parse loop). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/reference_impl.md

internal/git/log.go — the SHIPPED dependency (P1.M3.T4.S1): RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error) returns []string of TRIMMED subjects, newest-first, empties dropped, nil on an unborn repo. Confirms isDuplicate's `subjects` argument is pre-trimmed (so isDuplicate is a pure ==). ALSO the package-doc-DEFERRAL precedent: a sibling method file using a PLAIN `package git` line + adjacent file-level comment deferring the // Package git doc to git.go (verified gofmt -l + go vet clean) — dedupe.go mirrors this for package generate. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/internal/git/log.go

Apply the identical structure for TestFirstLine / TestIsDuplicate. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/internal/provider/parse_test.go

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/research/P1_M6_T1_S2_research.md — full research: the two functions' exact semantics, the trim-responsibility boundary, the empirically-verified edge-case matrix (single/multi-line, whitespace-trim, CRLF, empty, nil, near/paraphrase, case-difference, internal-whitespace-difference), the table-test convention, and the package-doc deferral precedent. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/research/P1_M6_T1_S2_research.md
strings.IndexByte — finds the first '\n' for firstLine's head-1 behavior (allocation-free; preferred over strings.Split). https://pkg.go.dev/strings#IndexByte
strings.TrimSpace — trims leading/trailing whitespace (incl. '\r', '\t', spaces, '\n') from firstLine's first line, so CRLF and padded subjects are handled. https://pkg.go.dev/strings#TrimSpace
